---
title: "Getting started with R"
date: 2026-06-21
categories: [basics]
description: "A first reproducible R chunk — proof the tutorials run."
---

Tutorials here are written in Quarto, so the R runs when the page is rendered
and the output is baked in. Here's a minimal example.

```{r}
# A tiny built-in dataset: average measurements of three iris species.
species_means <- aggregate(
  Sepal.Length ~ Species,
  data = iris,
  FUN  = mean
)

species_means
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-sepal
#| fig-cap: "Mean sepal length by species."
barplot(
  height    = species_means$Sepal.Length,
  names.arg = species_means$Species,
  col       = "#0077B6",
  ylab      = "Sepal length (cm)"
)
```

To add a tutorial, copy this folder, rename it, and write. Code style here
follows the [r-best-practice](https://github.com/wurli/r-best-practice)
conventions: `snake_case` names, aligned arguments, and self-contained chunks.
